---
title: "Final Project: Financial News Sentiment & Regional Bank Volatility"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Overview

This project asks:

1. Can LLM-derived sentiment signals from financial news predict short-term volatility in regional bank stocks?
2. Do sentiment indicators provide earlier or stronger signals than traditional market metrics?
3. Are market reactions geographically concentrated?

## Data

- Market data: `yfinance` (regional bank tickers + `KRE`)
- News/regulatory text: (planned) `GDELT` / `EDGAR` / other public source
- Geography: bank HQ location + US state shapefile

### Folder conventions

- Raw inputs go in `data/raw-data/`
- Derived/clean tables go in `data/derived-data/`

## Reproducibility

Run preprocessing to (re)build derived datasets:

```bash
python preprocessing.py
```

## Results

### Static Plot 1 (Altair): Time series with sentiment overlay

*(to be implemented)*

### Static Plot 2 (Spatial): US map of stress indicators

*(to be implemented)*

## Streamlit App

The Streamlit app lives in `streamlit-app/`. See `README.md` for how to run it locally.
